LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS RANDY NORTH AND SUE YU'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. FIRST, THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED AND ACCORDING TO THE PROPER PROCEDURE, SEE CRC 3.1700(A)(1)). SECOND, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE $13.00 PRINTING FEE AND COPYING FEE [CCP 1033.5(B)(3)]; THE $1050.00 LEGAL RESEARCH FEES [CCP 1033.5(A)(10) AND (B)(2)]; AND THE $1200 REAL ESTATE RESEARCH FEE [CCP 1033.5(B)(2)]. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FILING FEE FOR THE DEMURRER [CCP 1032(A)(1)] AND THE $250.00 MEDIATION FEE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION AS TO THE $740.00 FILING FEE. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. DEFENDANT MAY WISH TO SUBMIT ITS OWN. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-26-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Ntc And Motion For Atty Fees
Off Calendar Jul-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS DMITRIY TOCHILNIK AND ANNA KOVAL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES.

APPEARANCES: Randy North, 673-0189 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Dmitriy Tochilnik, 624-9703 deft & moving party in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED with prejudice. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Ntc And Motion For Atty Fees
Off Calendar Jun-28-2011 Continued To Jul-26-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DMITRIY TOCHILNIK, ANNA KOVAL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to July 26, 2011. Plaintiff to provide courtesy copy of its opposition to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar Jun-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS RANDY NORTH AND SUE YU'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS.

APPEARANCES: Randy North & Sue Yu, 673-0189 pltfs & moving parties in propria persona; Dmitriy Tochilnik & Anna Koval, 624-9703 defts & opposing parties in propria persona.

RULING: Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. FIRST, THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED AND ACCORDING TO THE PROPER PROCEDURE, SEE CRC 3.1700(A)(1)). SECOND, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE $13.00 PRINTING FEE AND COPYING FEE (CCP 1033.5(B)(3); the $1050.00 LEGAL RESEARCH FEES (CCP 1033.5(A)(10) AND (B)(2)); AND THE $1200 REAL ESTATE RESEARCH FEE (CCP 1033.5(B)(2)). THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FILING FEE FOR THE DEMURRER (CCP 1032(A)(1)) AND THE $250.00 MEDIATION FEE. In addition, the Court denied the motion as to the $740.00 filing fee. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. Defendant may wish to submit its own. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS DMITRIY TOCHILNIK AND ANNA KOVAL'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Richard Paris, Esq. 456-0678 for pltfs & opposing parties; Alexander Volchegursky, Esq. of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP 392-2560 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: DEMURRER OVERRULED. Defendants shall answer the first amended complaint. Order signed in open court. (Note: Court ruled on a previous demurrer sustaining with leave to amend. Once amended complaint filed, defendants to answer within the statutory time.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ST. FRANCIS BAY - ONE HUNDRED ONE CRESCENT WAY CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Houri Parsi, Esq. 456-0678 for pltfs & opposing parties; Anand R. Upadhye, Esq. of Berding & Weil LLP 925/838-2090 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 6th and 8th causes of action to plead provisions of the Davis-Sterling Act that defendant violated. Demurrer overruled as to remainder. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
